title: Tsukuyomi Blue Glass Dip Pen
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Office Instruments > Writing & Drawing Instruments > Pens & Pencils > Pens
description: Tsukuyomi is the moon god in Japanese mythology and is revered as the god who rules the night. This glass pen was created by glass artisan Rika to dedicate to Tsukuyomi. Inside the glass pen is an opal floating on a series of pillars, designed with the concept of enclosing a gemstone crystallized by the light of the moon. The surface of the glass pen is sprayed with silver to further emphasize the fantastic atmosphere. Each stroke of this pen is not merely writing but an act of devotion to the moon's serene beauty. The appeal of her work lies not just in its beautiful design and concept. Her pen nibs are incredibly smooth, almost eliminating any dragging sensation. This is one of the reasons why many fans of Japanese glass pens are avid supporters of her creations. We also want you to know her not only as an artisan but also as a person. Please look at 'The Story of SOURIRE' below and feel her charm. Explore our Japanese glass dip pens collection, each meticulously handcrafted by highly skilled artisans. Details Brand: SOURIRE Quantity: 1 Pen (with a pen cap) Length: 6.1" (15.5 cm) Weight(Pen): 1oz (28 g) Nib Size(Approximate): Fine Material: Glass Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. The Story of SOURIRE After graduating from an art university, Rika Suga began creating glass artworks in 2004. Her creations, born from a unique sensibility, encapsulate a romantic worldview. Her cheerful and approachable nature adds to her charm in contrast to her focused demeanor during production. SOURIRE, which means 'smile' in French, captures the essence of Rika Suga's atelier in the heart of Japan in Shinshu Azumino. Surrounded by the Japanese Alps, the atelier offers serene views of Mt. Ariake. Rika draws inspiration from the ever-changing scenes of the mountains, the clarity of the air, and the purity of flowing waters, creating pieces that aim to bring a smile to those who encounter them. Her craftsmanship is also exceptional, particularly noticeable in the smooth glide of the pen nib. Japanese glass pens are often of high quality, offering a smooth writing experience with minimal drag, but her creations are particularly notable for their exceptional smoothness.

The Story of FLAME WORKS Meet Hideki Hosaka, a self-taught wonder in the world of glass sculpting. His creations, known for their exquisite delicacy and beauty, are special. Hideki's little corner of creativity, FLAME WORKS, is tucked away in Chiba Prefecture, right next to the bustling city of Tokyo. His journey into the magical world of glass began in high school and, interestingly, continued even as he ventured down a different career path. This blend of dedication and love for his craft shines through in every piece he makes. The 'Magic Rose' and 'Spear of Longinus' glass works, which he was commissioned to produce in conjunction with the release of major motion pictures such as the live-action "Beauty and the Beast" and "Evangelion," are symbols of his widely recognized artistry and skill. Each creation from FLAME WORKS is a unique treasure, brimming with Hideki's personal touch and artistic vision. Imagine having one of his creations on your desk – it’s like having a little piece of art to call your own! His pieces blend beauty with practicality so that they’re sure to bring a new level of inspiration to your workspace.

description: Crafted with an artful blend of tradition and fantasy, the "Emerald Smoke" glass pen by Synchronicity Glass Art is a mesmerizing piece of functional art. Each pen is born from the skilled hands of Ryuu Oota, a master glass artist whose dedication to the craft since 2005 has culminated in these exceptional writing instruments. The "Emerald Smoke" design captures the essence of smoke tendrils encased within a durable, heat-resistant glass body. The smoke effect is created not through dyes but with a unique fuming technique that employs pure gold and silver, heated to just the right temperature to evoke ethereal colors without compromising the glass's clarity. In holding an "Emerald Smoke" pen, one is not simply wielding a tool but an artifact that bridges the gap between everyday utility and the pursuit of beauty. It is Synchronicity Glass Art's heartfelt wish that this pen brings a sense of happiness and a breath of creativity to your writing experience. The Story of Synchronicity Glass Art Since 2005, Synchronicity Glass Art, a studio led by the talented glass artist Ryu Ota, has been captivating imaginations with their heat-resistant glass creations. Their specialty lies in the art of fuming, a technique that brings out mystical colors but instead utilizes the natural shades that emerge from gold and silver at high temperatures. This mastery of fuming allows them to craft accessories and objets d'art that are not unique but also exude gentle transparency and ethereal quality. Embracing the mesmerizing hues of dichroic glass, Synchronicity Glass Art has also excelled in cold-working techniques since 2012, further enhancing the intricate and fantastical appeal of their works. The essence of Synchronicity Glass Art's philosophy is embedded in each piece, reflecting a hope that those who hold their art feel an uplift in spirit, even if just a little. Every day, the studio is devoted to the pursuit of designs that resonate with the individuality of the artist, emphasizing the signature translucent and dreamlike colors that have become their hallmark. Synchronicity Glass Art stands as a realm where the fantastical world within the glass comes to life, offering a touch of happiness to anyone who comes across their magical creations.

The Story of Glass Kaoria Discover the world of Atelier Glass Kaoria, a gem within an hour's reach of Kyoto Station. Atelier Glass Kaoria is not just about observing art; it's about engaging with it. The founder herself leads workshops in making accessories, glass pens, and plates, offering a hands-on experience of her artistic process. So, if you're in Kyoto, don't miss the chance to immerse yourself in the captivating world of Glass Kaoria.

The Story of FLAME WORKS Meet Hideki Hosaka, a self-taught wonder in the world of glass sculpting. His creations, known for their exquisite delicacy and beauty, are special. title: Goldfish Aquarium Glass Dip Pen
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Office Instruments > Writing & Drawing Instruments > Pens & Pencils > Pens
description: This delightful glass pen features a charming little fishbowl at the tip, with playful red and orange goldfish that seem to swim merrily. The pen shaft, reminiscent of a fishbowl filled with water plants, combines with the fishbowl tip to create a truly unique glass pen experience. It's an enchanting piece that brings a touch of whimsy and aquatic beauty to your writing routine. While many glass pens in Japan are made from hard glass, Sanae Abe from Glass Studio a_bee creates all of her glass pens from soft glass. This material melts at relatively low temperatures and has high rates of expansion when heated and contraction when cooled, which makes uniform processing difficult and increases the likelihood of cracking during production. Sanae capitalizes on this propensity for non-uniformity, crafting pens where each shaft to tip varies slightly, resembling the fluidity of water, thus ensuring each pen has its own individual variance. Sanae embraces this individuality, eschewing mass production for a process that, while more time-consuming, celebrates the distinct beauty found in each creation. She invites you to partake in an experience closer to art than mere writing, to savor the distinct pleasure of a pen that is as much a masterpiece as a tool for expression. As you can see from the product photos, each piece has a notable degree of individuality, showcasing the unique variances that can occur. While it's a surprise which distinct personality each pen will possess upon arrival, rest assured that you will cherish the one-of-a-kind character it brings. Explore our Japanese glass dip pens collection, each meticulously handcrafted by highly skilled artisans. Details Brand: Glass Studio a_bee - Sanae Abe- Komorebi Original Design Quantity: 1 pen Length(Pen): 5.5" - 6.3" (14 - 16 cm) Weight(Pen): Approximately 1.4 oz (40 g) Nib Size(Approximate): Fine Material: Glass Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Discover Glass Studio a_bee Glass Studio a_bee stands proudly near Sayamagaoka station in Saitama with its charming bee logo. It was founded by Sanae Abe, who was born in Tokyo's heart at Nihonbashi. She entered the creative world through her studies in visual communication design at the prestigious Musashino Art University. After university, Sanae thrived as a graphic designer in advertising but yearned to shape her artistic vision rather than meet commercial needs. Thus, in 2014, she embarked on crafting lampwork beads. While she learned glass sculpting in classes held by a Japanese glass material manufacturer, her career and skills as a glass artisan are largely self-taught, which is why her pieces are so distinctive, reflecting her individuality. Her designs, especially the glass pens encapsulating miniature aquariums, tell this story. What sets her work apart is her choice of soft glass over the more commonly used hard glass by many artisans. Despite its challenges, like a higher expansion coefficient leading to increased fragility with temperature changes and more variation between pieces, Sanae's efforts yield flexibility in design that simulates the enchanting effect of fish trapped in an aquarium. Why not experience the uniqueness of her glass pens firsthand? Each piece is a testament to Sanae's unique blend of art and craftsmanship.

title: Hand-Carved Wide Wagatabon Japanese Chestnut Pen Tray
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Filing & Organization > Desk Organizers
description: This special pen tray exudes warmth with its hand-carved texture, evoking a true sense of craftsmanship. This tray is ideally sized for arranging multiple fountain pens on your desk. Wagatabon, a Japanese craft, is meticulously created by artisans who first shape planks from logs and then carve them by hand. Originating from Ishikawa Prefecture, a region known for its Wajima lacquer used on fountain pens, the Wagatabon harmonizes beautifully with the sophisticated textures of these premium pens. Wagatabon trays were traditionally crafted for personal use during the Edo to Meiji periods in the village of Wagata in Ishikawa Prefecture. It’s said that the local craftsmen, who specialized in creating chestnut wood shingles for thatched roofs, turned to crafting these trays as a side business. As the roofing demand shifted from thatch to tile, the need for these chestnut shingles waned, gradually losing the artisans and their techniques. The village eventually submerged under the Wagatani Dam in 1959, rendering the Wagatabon trays a mythical artifact of the past. Now, based on the surviving historical documents, a handful of woodworking artisans have revived these trays. The careful and delicate carving marks each piece with the warmth of human touch, and its pared-down, simple form adds to its allure. For a tray to hold your cherished pens, consider this legendary piece, revived from history and brought quietly into the light. Explore our collection of Japanese pen trays, where timeless craftsmanship meets modern elegance. Details Brand: Komorebi Stationery - Masayoshi Kanematsu Quantity: 1 tray Size: 8.6×7.1×0.8inches / 22×18×2cm Inner Dimensions: 7.9×6.3inches / 20×16cm Weight: 6oz / 170g Material: Japanese Chestnut Note: 1. Each piece is hand-carved from natural logs, so variations in color and wood grain are to be expected. 2. Since natural wood is used, warping may occur due to humidity, dryness, direct sunlight, or exposure to air conditioning. Please store the tray upside down for proper care. Explore the Journey of Masayoshi Kanematsu Nestled in the town of Akeno, surrounded by the majestic Japanese Alps and bathed in the nation's longest sunlight hours, lies the workshop of Masayoshi Kanematsu. Masayoshi, a Wagatabon artisan, began woodworking around 30, crafting furniture and household goods while working in semiconductor equipment development in a corporate setting. After retiring in 2016, Masayoshi's journey took a serious turn towards becoming a dedicated woodworking craftsman. His love for Wagatabon sparked in November 2016 when he was captivated by its simple yet beautiful aesthetic on television. Wagatabon, a craft that had once faded into obscurity, became his passion. Drawing from magazines and internet resources, he started creating his unique take on the craft. By 2019, under the tutelage of Shinichi Moriguchi, a leading figure in the revival of Wagatabon, Masayoshi honed his creation methods. Since then, he has been committed to spreading the allure of the Wagatabon craft, dedicating himself to the art of making these exquisite pieces.

title: Gold Leaf Sakura and Bird Stationery Box with Red Felt Interior
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Filing & Organization > Desk Organizers
description: This stationery box is a stunning example of traditional Japanese artistry, adorned with gold leaf over a checkered pattern symbolic of good fortune. On the lid, a tranquil scene unfolds with cherry blossoms and birds reveling in the spring air, beautifully rendered in Hakuichi's signature relief technique to create a slightly raised, three-dimensional effect. Beyond its exquisite exterior, the box reveals a rich red felt interior upon opening. The box itself and the inside of the lid are lined with felt, providing a soft cushion that ensures your treasured items remain scratch-free. I use this box to store an A5-sized notebook alongside my favorite fountain pen. The box’s substantial presence enhances the desk’s aesthetic, and even the simple act of opening the lid to retrieve my notebook and pen becomes a moment that uplifts my spirit. This exquisite box was presented as a gift from Japan to the United States during the Japan-US summit held in February 2017 and is highly regarded as an excellent choice for a gift. Details Brand: Hakuichi Quantity: 1 Box Size: 8.5 " x 11.5" x 2.6" (21.7 cm × 29.4 cm × 6.6cm) Weight: 40 oz (1,130 g) Material: Gold Leaf on MDF Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Discover Hakuichi's Artistry Hakuichi, located in Kanazawa City in Ishikawa Prefecture — where 99% of Japan's gold leaf is produced — has been a pioneering force in gold leaf artistry since 1975. As a leader in this field, Hakuichi has been crucial in popularizing and preserving this traditional Japanese craft globally. Kanazawa's fame in gold leaf production is deeply rooted in its artisans' exceptional craftsmanship, characterized by precision and perseverance. In the Edo period, when gold beating was restricted outside Edo (Tokyo) and Kyoto, Kanazawa's artisans refined their craft under challenging conditions. This period of adversity ultimately led to the region becoming renowned for its exquisite gold leaf. With each piece of gold leaf stationery, Hakuichi brings a touch of Kanazawa's elegance to your desk. These products are not merely tools for writing but embody a rich legacy of Japanese artistry. High-quality gold in their stationery transforms every writing experience into a luxurious encounter, making these items highly valued among Japanese traditional crafts and a splendid addition for any stationery enthusiast.

description: Upon handling this bamboo pen tray, one is immediately struck by its substantial weight and warmth—a testament to its quality. Adorned with Kanazawa gold leaf, a traditional Japanese craft, its surface glistens with the refined beauty of this age-old technique. The tray comfortably holds up to three to five of your favorite pens with room to spare, yet it's thoughtfully sized never to overwhelm your desk space. Bamboo, an integral part of Japanese history, is celebrated for its flexibility and durability. It has been utilized in everything from weaponry like arrows and bows to everyday items such as baskets, and even as a construction material for houses. Combining bamboo's historical significance with the luxurious touch of Kanazawa gold leaf, it has a substantial thickness that adds a discreet yet unmistakable presence, perfect for organizing your desk. Originally serving as a tea tray, it is also ideally sized for a cup or small snacks, ensuring versatility and elegance in its usage. Explore our collection of Japanese pen trays, where timeless craftsmanship meets modern elegance. Details Brand: Hakuichi Quantity: 1 pen tray Size: 9×9×0.5inches / 23×13 ×1.4cm Weight: 10.5 oz / 300 g Material: Gold Leaf on bamboo Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Discover Hakuichi's Artistry Hakuichi, located in Kanazawa City in Ishikawa Prefecture — where 99% of Japan's gold leaf is produced — has been a pioneering force in gold leaf artistry since 1975. As a leader in this field, Hakuichi has been crucial in popularizing and preserving this traditional Japanese craft globally. Kanazawa's fame in gold leaf production is deeply rooted in its artisans' exceptional craftsmanship, characterized by precision and perseverance. In the Edo period, when gold beating was restricted outside Edo (Tokyo) and Kyoto, Kanazawa's artisans refined their craft under challenging conditions. This period of adversity ultimately led to the region becoming renowned for its exquisite gold leaf. With each piece of gold leaf stationery, Hakuichi brings a touch of Kanazawa's elegance to your desk. These products are not merely tools for writing but embody a rich legacy of Japanese artistry. High-quality gold in their stationery transforms every writing experience into a luxurious encounter, making these items highly valued among Japanese traditional crafts and a splendid addition for any stationery enthusiast.

title: Urushi Kohako Plum Lacquer Trinket Box
google_product_category: Home & Garden > Household Supplies > Storage & Organization
description: The small box is beautifully adorned with lacquer and traditional Japanese auspicious patterns. The Ume pattern, with its plum blossoms, symbolizes resilience and renewal. The plum blossom, blooming in the cold of winter, is a herald of spring, embodying hope and the strength to overcome adversity. The combination of Japan's celebrated lacquerware and traditional motifs has been cherished in Japan for ages, bringing a touch of Japanese sentiment to your desk. This versatile box can hold your trinkets or even serve as a dish for sweets and desserts. Muneaki Hayashi, an artisan known for his work in Echizen lacquerware from Fukui, meticulously applies the lacquer to this box. Lacquerware production involves a detailed division of labor among artisans who mold the base, apply the lacquer, and paint the designs. Classified as a corner lacquerer, Hayashi's skilled craftsmanship is evident in the box's exterior lacquer application. The interior is coated with urethane, making it resistant to cracking even after washing, allowing you to use and clean it with ease. it is a piece where Japanese aesthetics and tradition come to life. By incorporating it into your daily life, you can experience the unique allure of lacquerware and the warmth of artisan craftsmanship. Why not add a touch of luxury and traditional Japanese beauty to your desk or room? Details Brand: isuke - Muneaki Hayashi Quantity: 1 Box Size: 3.6×3.6×2inches / 9.2×9.2 ×5.3cm Weight: 6.3oz / 180g Material: Lacquer on MDF Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Discover isuke's 190 years of History Isuke was founded approximately 190 years ago, around 1830, in Kyoto as a manufacturer of lacquer materials. Originally a manufacturer of lacquer coatings, Isuke has played a vital role in supporting the traditional craft of Japanese lacquerware by providing coatings to lacquer-producing regions throughout Japan. Leveraging its history and achievements, as well as its connections with artisans, Isuke has launched its brand, uniquely positioning itself as both a manufacturer of lacquer raw materials and a purveyor of lacquerware products. At a time when lacquerware is increasingly disappearing from the lives of younger generations in Japan, the Isuke brand has taken it as its mission to reintegrate lacquerware into daily life. While broadcasting the inherent allure of lacquerware— the warmth of wood, the moisture of lacquer, and the beauty that comes with age—Isuke also combines modern designs that appeal to the younger demographic, continuing to create wonderful products.

title: Red Imperial Chrysanthemum Tatami Pen Tray
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Filing & Organization > Desk Organizers
description: Our tatami pen tray is graced with a chrysanthemum pattern, akin to the one used in the Imperial family's crest. This symbol of nobility is reimagined in delightful, approachable colors, adding a casual yet distinguished charm to your desk. It's a design that celebrates tradition with a playful twist, perfect for adding a touch of Japan's esteemed culture to your everyday. Tatami mats have graced Japanese estates since the Heian period (8th to 12th centuries), signifying wealth among nobles and warriors. Today, they continue to be a staple in Japanese homes and temples, perhaps you've even seen them on your travels. The scent of rush grass from tatami is wonderfully comforting, and their softness is unmatched for rest and relaxation. This has made tatami rooms a cherished feature in even the most modern Japanese homes. Many have noticed tatami's golden hue, but it begins as a vibrant green, gracefully maturing with sunlight. Our pen tray invites you to savor this natural aging process, bringing a piece of Japanese life to your desk, as the colors and textures gently shift over time. Browse our Japanese pen trays and pen rests, combining tradition with modern elegance. Details Brand: Sasasho Quantity: 1 pen tray Size: 7.8×3.9×0.4inches / 20×10 ×1cm Weight: 3.9oz / 110g Material: Rush Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Discover Sasasho For over 40 years, Sasasho has played a pivotal role in supporting the local homes and businesses, delivering tatami that underpins the regional and Japanese way of life and culture. Yasuyuki Sasaki, the current president, proudly represents the second generation at the helm of this essential local enterprise. Sasasho isn't just about making tatami; they're also the local heroes when it comes to repairing fusuma, shoji, and mosquito screens, ensuring every Japanese home feels just right. Embracing tradition while welcoming innovation, they've ventured into crafting tatami mats with unique designs and safe tatami blocks for kids, proving that tradition can evolve and still stay true to its roots. For those enchanted by Japanese culture and lifestyle, Sasasho offers a glimpse into how traditional craftsmanship like theirs continues to adapt and thrive. It's a testament to how they're blending the old with the new, inviting us all to appreciate the craftsmanship that has supported Japanese homes and culture for decades.

title: Kimono Beauty Series Rose Iyo Washi Tape
google_product_category: Arts & Entertainment > Hobbies & Creative Arts > Arts & Crafts > Art & Crafting Materials > Crafting Adhesives & Magnets > Decorative Tape
description: This washi tape boasts elegant roses on a blue backdrop, each bloom carefully rendered in hues of purple and lavender. Gold lines accentuate the design, catching the light and adding a luxurious flourish. White roses, frequently depicted on kimonos, are emblems of purity and innocence. Crafted in Shikoku Chuo City, where clear streams have nurtured the art of paper-making, Kamiiso's washi tape honors the legacy of Iyo Washi paper that began in the hands of local farmers about 250 years ago. Amidst the global spread of 'Washi Tape,' where many variants do not employ real washi, they commit to authenticity with the warm texture of true Iyo Washi. This tape is not just a stationery item; it's a touch of Japanese tradition, designed to bring a Japanese elegance to your daily routine and make each moment at your desk a little more delightful. Let this tape inspire your creativity and add joy to your daily life! Browse our Japanese washi tapes, effortlessly adorable. Details Brand: Kamiiso Quantity: 1 Roll Size: 0.6inches × 23feet / 15mm × 7m Material: Iyo Washi (Iyo Japanese Paper) Kamiiso: A Century of Washi Excellence Kamiiso, nestled in the heart of Shikoku Chuo City, Ehime Prefecture, has been a bastion of traditional Iyo Washi paper since its foundation in 1926. What began as a wholesaler delivering the allure of Iyo Washi across Japan has, over the decades, blossomed into an esteemed manufacturer, especially after expanding into the production of specialized paper for kimono storage around 30 years post-establishment. Today, Shikoku Chuo City is revered as Japan's leading hub for paper goods, thanks in no small part to Kamiiso's dedication to spreading this time-honored tradition. Adapting to the changing eras and the evolving lifestyles of the Japanese people, Kamiiso has diversified its offerings. The company now energetically produces a range of paper products, including table mats, coasters, and notably, their much-loved masking tapes. In an era where many washi tapes no longer contain real washi (Japanese paper), Kamiiso stands apart. They apply a century's worth of spun techniques and steadfast quality to their Washi Tapes, captivating stationery enthusiasts worldwide with their genuine dedication to this authentic Japanese craft.

Sakura also carries meaning, representing new beginnings, and is a common motif on kimonos worn at the start of Japan's new fiscal year. This tape embodies a design that reflects Japanese traditions and the changing of the seasons. Crafted in Shikoku Chuo City, where clear streams have nurtured the art of paper-making, Kamiiso's washi tape honors the legacy of Iyo Washi paper that began in the hands of local farmers about 250 years ago. Amidst the global spread of 'Washi Tape,' where many variants do not employ real washi, they commit to authenticity with the warm texture of true Iyo Washi. This tape is not just a stationery item; it's a touch of Japanese tradition, designed to bring a Japanese elegance to your daily routine and make each moment at your desk a little more delightful. Let this tape inspire your creativity and add joy to your daily life! Browse our Japanese washi tapes, effortlessly adorable. What began as a wholesaler delivering the allure of Iyo Washi across Japan has, over the decades, blossomed into an esteemed manufacturer, especially after expanding into the production of specialized paper for kimono storage around 30 years post-establishment. description: This washi tape features a sophisticated black background adorned with colorful butterflies and sakura. In Japan, the butterfly, which transforms from a chrysalis into a beautiful form, symbolizes growth, change, and beauty, and is often incorporated into kimono patterns. Sakura also carries meaning, representing new beginnings, and is a common motif on kimonos worn at the start of Japan's new fiscal year. This tape embodies a design that reflects Japanese traditions and the changing of the seasons. Crafted in Shikoku Chuo City, where clear streams have nurtured the art of paper-making, Kamiiso's washi tape honors the legacy of Iyo Washi paper that began in the hands of local farmers about 250 years ago. Amidst the global spread of 'Washi Tape,' where many variants do not employ real washi, they commit to authenticity with the warm texture of true Iyo Washi. This tape is not just a stationery item; it's a touch of Japanese tradition, designed to bring a Japanese elegance to your daily routine and make each moment at your desk a little more delightful. Let this tape inspire your creativity and add joy to your daily life! Browse our Japanese washi tapes, effortlessly adorable. Details Brand: Kamiiso Quantity: 1 Roll Size(Slim): 0.6inches × 23inches / 15mm × 7m Size(Wide): 1inches × 16.4feet / 15mm × 7m Material: Iyo Washi (Iyo Japanese Paper) Kamiiso: A Century of Washi Excellence Kamiiso, nestled in the heart of Shikoku Chuo City, Ehime Prefecture, has been a bastion of traditional Iyo Washi paper since its foundation in 1926. What began as a wholesaler delivering the allure of Iyo Washi across Japan has, over the decades, blossomed into an esteemed manufacturer, especially after expanding into the production of specialized paper for kimono storage around 30 years post-establishment. Today, Shikoku Chuo City is revered as Japan's leading hub for paper goods, thanks in no small part to Kamiiso's dedication to spreading this time-honored tradition. Adapting to the changing eras and the evolving lifestyles of the Japanese people, Kamiiso has diversified its offerings. The company now energetically produces a range of paper products, including table mats, coasters, and notably, their much-loved masking tapes. In an era where many washi tapes no longer contain real washi (Japanese paper), Kamiiso stands apart. They apply a century's worth of spun techniques and steadfast quality to their Washi Tapes, captivating stationery enthusiasts worldwide with their genuine dedication to this authentic Japanese craft.

title: Raden and Gold Leaf Sakura Fuji Pen Box
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Filing & Organization > Desk Organizers
description: Korindo, inheriting the development and tradition of Echizen lacquerware, has crafted this Pen Box to make tradition and beauty accessible to everyone. The surface is coated with urethane, over which Mount Fuji and Sakura are depicted in mother-of-pearl inlay and gold leaf. Mother-of-pearl, now popular in fountain pen decorations and jewelry, has been frequently used by lacquerware artisans for decoration in Japanese history. The pen box becomes stunningly beautiful thanks to the mother-of-pearl inlays of Mount Fuji, which shimmer in various colors depending on the angle, and the gold leaf Sakura. The inside of the box is lined with felt, ensuring your precious pens are not scratched. It serves well as a storage box for your important stationery, but it is also perfect for adding a touch of Japanese beauty to your desk. And for you, who values not just the design but also the background, history, and traditions behind it, using this will surely bring satisfaction every time. Details Brand: Korindo Quantity: 1 Box Size: 8.2×4×2Inches / 20.8×10×5cm Weight: 11.8oz / 335g Material: Gold Leaf on Urethane Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Discover the Backstory of Korindo Founded in 1938 in Fukui Prefecture and boasting a history of over 80 years, Korindo has gained recognition for its significant role, including providing ceremonial tableware for the enthronement of Japan's new emperor. "Korindo" is their brand name, formally known as Echizen Lacquerware Co., Ltd., which plays a vital role in inheriting the traditional craft of Echizen lacquerware, a quintessence of Japanese artisanship. Their corporate philosophy, "Brilliance of Rin to the world," draws inspiration from Rin, a leading school of Japanese art and culture during the medieval era, still influential today. Being close to Kyoto, the capital at the time, has deeply rooted their work in Rin's influence, incorporating its patterns into their lacquerware. Embracing Rin's spirit of freedom, boldness, and challenge, combined with the craftsmanship passed down through generations, they aim to create lacquerware that enriches lives and brings joy. Representative paintings of Rin Japanese lacquer products, including fountain pens and tableware, are highly popular worldwide. Enjoy the exquisite offerings from Korindo, a bearer of the legacy of Echizen lacquerware, and immerse yourself in the rich history embedded in their products.

Details Brand: Shogado Quantity: 1 Size: 2×22.8inches / 5×58cm Weight: 20g Material: Paper Tradition and Techniques of Shogado Since its establishment in Kyoto in 1964, Shogado has evolved from selling traditional Japanese bound books to creating stationery and miscellaneous goods crafted from washi paper. This transition reflects their expertise in utilizing authentic washi sourced from renowned production areas like Fukui Prefecture. Particularly noteworthy are the products hand-dyed by skilled artisans in Kyoto, known as Yuzen paper, distinguished by their superior texture and coloration, setting them apart from mass-produced goods. Shogado's philosophy is deeply rooted in carrying forward the Japanese aesthetic to future generations. Just as traditional bound books once played a pivotal role in people's daily lives in an analog Japan, Shogado continues to innovate products that meet the demands of the modern era. At Komorebi Stationery, we are delighted to offer these exquisite items, inviting you to experience the charm of Shogado's craftsmanship.

title: Sanzen Tomoe River FP Hardcover Notebook A5 368 Pages
google_product_category: Office Supplies > General Office Supplies > Paper Products > Notebooks & Notepads
description: This reknowned Japanese notebook with a hard cover uses Tomoe River, which is exceptionally compatible with fountain pens and renowned worldwide. Its resistance to bleeding and feathering when writing with fountain pens is a distinctive feature, earning it the support of fountain pen lovers and calligraphers. Another significant characteristic of the Tomoe River FP, born from paper paper, is its grid. At first glance, it appears to be made of ordinary dots or grids, but it is composed of microdots that are invisible to the naked eye, greatly reducing ink bleed-through. This technology called "New Sub Dot Grid" has also been internationally patented, contributing to the popularity of Tomoe River FP. The notebook's cover features a hard cover based on black with a sophisticated design, making it suitable for both work and personal use. If you've ever wanted to write on quality paper, this is definitely a product you should try. Explore our Japanese notebooks collection, crafted for exceptional writing and creative sketching. Details Size: A5 - 5.8"×8.26" (14.8 cm × 21 cm) Pages: 368 Sheet Type: Blank / Dot Grid / Grid Paper Type: Tomoe River FP Paper Weight: 52 gsm Weight: 13.9 oz (395 g) Brand: paper paper The History of Tomoe River FP Notebook’s Maker Paper Paper is a brand created by SAKAE Technical Paper. Its history dates back to 1940. SAKAE has gone through several successions and company splits. It has also been an agent for Tomoe River, a brand with a global fanbase. Tomoe River’s success likely owes much to SAKAE’s achievements. Since 2014, SAKAE has produced and sold Tomoe RiverFP notebooks using Tomoe River paper. Even after Tomoegawa transferred the rights to Sanzen Seishi, SAKAE continued production using the new Tomoe River. The mission of Paper Paper is to explore the appeal of paper and expand possibilities between people and paper. Beyond Tomoe River, they continue to develop new papers like Iroful, supporting stationery lovers worldwide.

title: Red Pine Wooden Page Notebook A6
google_product_category: Office Supplies > General Office Supplies > Paper Products > Notebooks & Notepads
description: Once you open the cover of the Wooden Page Softcover Notebook, you’ll find pages made from thinly shaved and dried wood, known as “Kyogi” (経木). The wood’s texture and grain patterns bring a serene feeling every time you open the notebook. Kyogi has an interesting history in Japan. About 1500 years ago, when Buddhism was introduced, paper was rare and precious. Monks at that time wrote sutras on thin wood as part of their training. The technology back then couldn’t shave wood as thinly as today. The manufacturer, Yamatowa, welcomed skilled artisans to develop the technique. They succeeded in commercializing this unique Japanese notebook. Since it’s made of 100% wood, ink may bleed or transfer easily. If that concerns you, I recommend using a pencil or ballpoint pen. Sketching with colored pencils on the wood is also a great idea. Alternatively, you can embrace the ink bleed as a natural feature of the wood. Enjoy it as part of the notebook’s unique charm. Wood may warp due to humidity. It may also change color over time as it ages. Please enjoy these natural changes as part of your notebook’s journey. For a larger drawing and sketching surface, see our Red Pine Wooden Page Notebook B6. Explore our Japanese notebooks collection, crafted for exceptional writing and creative sketching. Details Size: A6 - 4.1" × 5.8" (10.5 cm × 14.8 cm) Sheet Material: Natural Wood (Japanese Red Pine) Sheet thickness: 0.2 mm Pages: 100 Sheet Type: Blank Weight: 3.5 oz (100 g) Brand: Shiki bun Coexistence with the Forest of Shiki Bun In the Ina Valley, located in Nagano Prefecture in Japan, a company called Yamatowa supports coexistence with forests. Once, people were closely involved with forests and received many blessings from them. However, as lifestyles changed and forests were left untended, overgrown and dark forests increased. Untrimmed branches and leaves block sunlight, damaging the health and ecosystem of the forest. Yamatowa believes that although it's difficult to return to the old ways of living, it's possible to bring living closer to forests again and increase the number of healthy and bright forests that are well-maintained, as in the past. They think that using Japanese wood appropriately in daily life will lead to forest conservation, and with this in mind, they launched a brand called Shiki. To realize their vision, they use Akamatsu pine from the Ina Valley, and skilled woodworkers create products including notebooks and other wooden products.

title: Red Pine Wooden Page Notebook B6
google_product_category: Office Supplies > General Office Supplies > Paper Products > Notebooks & Notepads
description: Every page in this red pine notebook is made from real wood. Not paper inspired by wood, not paper printed with wood grain, but actual Japanese red pine (Akamatsu), shaved into sheets thin enough to turn. This is the B6 size of our best-selling Red Pine Wooden Page Notebook A6. Many of you have asked us to bring back the B6 format, and after working closely with the manufacturer, we're pleased to offer it again. The larger surface gives you more room to sketch, draw, and write on a material unlike anything else in your collection. Unlike the A6, the B6 opens flat, so you can write and draw across the full spread without holding the pages down. The pages are made using a technique called Kyogi. Over 1,500 years ago in Japan, when paper was still rare and expensive, Buddhist monks wrote sutras on thin slices of wood. That tradition eventually faded as paper became widely available, but the craft of shaving wood into ultra-thin sheets survived in Nagano Prefecture. Today, the manufacturer Yamatowa, based in the Ina Valley, works with skilled artisans who have refined this ancient method into something you can hold in your hands. Each page carries the natural grain, texture, and subtle fragrance of red pine. No two pages look exactly the same. The wood grain creates a surface that responds differently to every pen, pencil, and brush stroke, which is why many of our customers use this notebook as a small canvas for colored pencil illustrations, ink sketches, and mixed-media artwork. Because these pages are 100% natural wood, ink from fountain pens will bleed and show through. This is not a flaw. Many of our customers actually love the way fountain pen ink interacts with the wood fibers, creating unexpected patterns and textures. For clean lines, a pencil, ballpoint pen, or colored pencils work best. Over time, the wood pages may change color slightly, and some pages may warp with humidity. These are signs that you're holding a natural material that continues to age and evolve, much like the forests where it originated. The cover is made from recycled paper, which reflects the manufacturer's broader commitment to sustainability. The red pine used for the pages comes from forest thinning in the Ina Valley. Thinning removes excess trees so the remaining forest gets enough sunlight and space to stay healthy. By purchasing this notebook, you're directly supporting a cycle that keeps Japan's mountain forests well-maintained and thriving. title: Kimono Beauty Series Kotobuki Chrysanthemum Iyo Washi Tape
google_product_category: Arts & Entertainment > Hobbies & Creative Arts > Arts & Crafts > Art & Crafting Materials > Crafting Adhesives & Magnets > Decorative Tape
description: This is a washi tape beautifully decorated with chrysanthemums in red and gold on a black background. Chrysanthemums, alongside Sakuras, are considered national flowers of Japan, and the chrysanthemum is also used as the Imperial seal. This flower holds significant importance in Japan. A feature of this Iyo washi tape is that the gold parts shimmer beautifully when they catch the light, adding a touch of Japanese elegance and glamour wherever it is applied. Crafted in Shikoku Chuo City, where clear streams have nurtured the art of paper-making, Kamiiso's washi tape honors the legacy of Iyo Washi paper that began in the hands of local farmers about 250 years ago. Amidst the global spread of 'Washi Tape,' where many variants do not employ real washi, they commit to authenticity with the warm texture of true Iyo Washi. This tape is not just a stationery item; it's a touch of Japanese tradition, designed to bring a Japanese elegance to your daily routine and make each moment at your desk a little more delightful. Let this tape inspire your creativity and add joy to your daily life! Browse our Japanese washi tapes, effortlessly adorable. Details Brand: Kamiiso Quantity: 1 Roll Size(Slim): 0.6inches × 23inches / 15mm × 7m Size(Wide): 1inches × 16.4feet / 15mm × 7m Material: Iyo Washi (Iyo Japanese Paper) Kamiiso: A Century of Washi Excellence Kamiiso, nestled in the heart of Shikoku Chuo City, Ehime Prefecture, has been a bastion of traditional Iyo Washi paper since its foundation in 1926. What began as a wholesaler delivering the allure of Iyo Washi across Japan has, over the decades, blossomed into an esteemed manufacturer, especially after expanding into the production of specialized paper for kimono storage around 30 years post-establishment. Today, Shikoku Chuo City is revered as Japan's leading hub for paper goods, thanks in no small part to Kamiiso's dedication to spreading this time-honored tradition. Adapting to the changing eras and the evolving lifestyles of the Japanese people, Kamiiso has diversified its offerings. The company now energetically produces a range of paper products, including table mats, coasters, and notably, their much-loved masking tapes. In an era where many washi tapes no longer contain real washi (Japanese paper), Kamiiso stands apart. They apply a century's worth of spun techniques and steadfast quality to their Washi Tapes, captivating stationery enthusiasts worldwide with their genuine dedication to this authentic Japanese craft.

Details Size: B6 - 5.0" × 7.1" (12.8cm × 18.2 cm) Pages: 192 Pages (96 Sheets) Sheet Type: Dot Grid Paper Type: High-Quality Paper 70k Paper Weight: 81.4 gsm Weight: 9.9 oz (280 g) Material: Nishijin Textile(polyester) / Paper Brand: Morisan Note: Please note that the cover design of each notebook may vary slightly, as it depends on the specific section of the Nishijin textile fabric that is used. Morisan: Preserving Nishijin Textile 'Kinran' in Japanese Notebook "Bringing Nishijin Brocade Closer to Everyday Life" In Japan, if you speak of tradition and history, Kyoto often comes to mind. It is the former capital and a hub of cultural heritage. Since 1921, Morisan has been a steadfast purveyor of Kyoto’s traditional craft, Nishijin textile. Among their offerings, Morisan specializes in an exquisite form of Nishijin brocade known as 'kinran.' This luxurious fabric incorporates gold threads. They are made by applying gold leaf to washi paper, slicing it into hair-thin strips, and wrapping these around threads. These are then intricately woven to create stunning patterns. Traditionally, kinran was used in garments such as Buddhist monks' robes and costumes for Japanese dolls, but the demand for such items has diminished over time. However, Morisan has embraced a mission to integrate Nishijin brocade into contemporary life, making it more accessible and enjoyable. By substituting silk with polyester to maintain affordability, and crafting a range of everyday items from notebooks to bags, Morisan is making this opulent fabric a part of modern living. We invite people around the world to enjoy Morisan's Japanese notebook, which embody the tradition of Kyoto's Nishijin textile artisans—a tradition that Morisan has proudly sustained for over a century.

Yasutaka Takeuchi: Master of Handcrafted Fountain Pen Yasutaka Takeuchi, born in 1981 in Mie Prefecture, is a craftsman who has inherited the essence of traditional Japanese calligraphy brushes, a skill passed down from his grandfather's generation. From a young age, Takeuchi harbored a deep admiration for brush artisans, leading him to apprentice under his uncle after graduating high school. Despite shifting to a stable office job upon marriage, his passion for craftsmanship never waned. Self-taught, Takeuchi embarked on creating brush prototypes from scratch. His journey into pen-making began serendipitously while researching turning techniques online, where he stumbled upon a pen that captured his imagination. Takeuchi applies the meticulous craftsmanship of traditional calligraphy brush making, carefully selecting materials and painstakingly shaping and polishing each piece by hand. His Japanese pens transcend mere writing instruments, becoming works of art that inspire creativity and innovation in their users. Each Japanese fountain pen by Yasutaka Takeuchi embodies the pinnacle of his craftsmanship, merging Japanese tradition with the artisan's dedication. Owning and using one is not just about enjoying a fine writing instrument—it’s about connecting with the deep traditions and spirit behind Japanese pens. More than just tools, Takeuchi's handcrafted creations are treasured additions to any collection, symbolizing the fusion of tradition and artisanal passion.

Maeju: Crafting with Lacquer Mastery Maeju Shikki, officially known as Maeju Shikki Co., Ltd., is a long-established Japanese lacquerware maker founded in 1939 in Yamanaka Onsen, Japan. Located in Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture, Yamanaka has been renowned for over 400 years as a center of lacquerware production, especially for its exceptional woodturning techniques (rokuro-biki). The lacquerware from this region is beloved for its graceful forms, deep, lustrous red and black tones, and smooth texture that fits comfortably in the hand. Growing alongside this rich tradition, Maeju played a key role in elevating Yamanaka to become Japan’s top lacquerware-producing region. With over 80 years of history, Maeju has earned its place as a prestigious name in Yamanaka lacquerware, dedicated to sharing the beauty of Japanese lacquer with the world.

description: This tray, coated with lacquer in a subdued dark red, is adorned with gold and silver leaf. Details Brand: Korindo Quantity: 1 Tray Size: 7.1" W × 4.7" L × 0.6" D / 18 W × 12 L× 1.5 D cm Weight: 3 oz / 85 g Material: Composite / Gold Leaf / Silver Leaf Coating: Lacquer Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Discover the Backstory of Korindo Founded in 1938 in Fukui Prefecture and boasting a history of over 80 years, Korindo has gained recognition for its significant role, including providing ceremonial tableware for the enthronement of Japan's new emperor. "Korindo" is their brand name, formally known as Echizen Lacquerware Co., Ltd., which plays a vital role in inheriting the traditional craft of Echizen lacquerware, a quintessence of Japanese artisanship. Their corporate philosophy, "Brilliance of Rin to the world," draws inspiration from Rin, a leading school of Japanese art and culture during the medieval era, still influential today. Being close to Kyoto, the capital at the time, has deeply rooted their work in Rin's influence, incorporating its patterns into their lacquerware. Embracing Rin's spirit of freedom, boldness, and challenge, combined with the craftsmanship passed down through generations, they aim to create lacquerware that enriches lives and brings joy. Representative paintings of Rin Japanese lacquer products, including fountain pens and tableware, are highly popular worldwide. Enjoy the exquisite offerings from Korindo, a bearer of the legacy of Echizen lacquerware, and immerse yourself in the rich history embedded in their products.

Their corporate philosophy, "Brilliance of Rin to the world," draws inspiration from Rin, a leading school of Japanese art and culture during the medieval era, still influential today. Being close to Kyoto, the capital at the time, has deeply rooted their work in Rin's influence, incorporating its patterns into their lacquerware. Embracing Rin's spirit of freedom, boldness, and challenge, combined with the craftsmanship passed down through generations, they aim to create lacquerware that enriches lives and brings joy. Representative paintings of Rin Japanese lacquer products, including fountain pens and tableware, are highly popular worldwide. Enjoy the exquisite offerings from Korindo, a bearer of the legacy of Echizen lacquerware, and immerse yourself in the rich history embedded in their products.

title: Tame-Nuri Black Echizen Lacquered Sakura Wood Pen Rest
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Filing & Organization > Desk Organizers
description: This pen rest, made from Sakura wood and coated with genuine lacquer, is an authentic piece of Echizen lacquerware. Despite its simple design, its beauty is undeniable. The technique called Tame-Nuri involves applying a layer of translucent black lacquer over red lacquer, allowing a hint of red to show through. One of the greatest charms of Tame-Nuri is that, over time, the black lacquer gradually wears away to reveal more of the underlying red, enhancing its beauty. In Japan, we often use the term "Wabi-Sabi" to describe the beauty of aging and imperfection. This pen rest perfectly embodies that concept of beauty through the passage of time. The pen rest features a slight indentation in the center, preventing your pen from rolling off. Enjoy this small piece of Japanese traditional artisanship alongside your favorite pen. title: Twilight Blossom Nishijin Silk Fountain Pen Case - Triple Slot - yurie
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Filing & Organization > Pen & Pencil Cases
description: Introducing the exquisite Nishijin Silk Pen Case, a testament to the unparalleled craftsmanship of Kyoto's traditional artisans. This pen case is crafted from 100% silk, offering a luxurious touch and a dazzling interplay of blue and purple hues that change with the angle of light. The intricate patterns are the result of meticulously dyed threads and gold leaf accents woven into the fabric, a process requiring exceptional skill and precision. Nishijin weaving dates back to the 5th and 6th centuries, originally crafted from pure silk for the nobility of the imperial court. While modern variations often incorporate polyester for affordability, this pen case remains true to its heritage, using only the finest silk to create a piece of timeless elegance. This masterpiece is the result of a collaboration between three distinguished creators: Konkan Shoten, a Kyoto silk shop with a 270-year legacy; Yurie, an artist dedicated to spreading the beauty of Japanese traditions; and Komorebi Stationery, a brand passionate about bringing Japanese artistry to the world. Yurie's meticulous attention to detail extends to the silk lining, which gently cleans your pen of oils and fingerprints every time you store it. The tassel’s end is adorned with a bead meticulously lacquered by a Wajima-nuri artisan, giving it a color depth that is simply mesmerizing. Beyond its soft, luxurious feel, we are particularly enamored with the remarkable detail and luminous colors that only silk can offer. The incredibly fine silk threads allow for patterns with breathtaking precision, akin to a high-resolution photograph. Each thread shines with a unique luster, creating vibrant hues that are simply unattainable with other fibers. For instance, the seemingly purple threads of this pen case are actually a masterful blend of red warp threads and blue, aqua, and white weft threads, producing a stunning optical effect that changes with the light. Encase your cherished fountain pen in this luxurious Nishijin Silk Pen Case, a treasure that promises to be a lifelong companion. Not just a protective accessory, it’s a piece of art that you'll want to keep and cherish forever. Details Brand: yurie & Konkan Shoten - Komorebi Original Design Quantity: 1 pen case Length: 6.8" L × 2.2" W (15 cm L × 10.5 cm W) *Fits pens up to approximately 5.7" (14.5 cm) in length. Weight: 0.9 oz (25 g) Material: 100% Silk Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Passing Down the Tradition of Nishijin Textile In the historic city of Kyoto, the Matsumoto family of Konkan Shoten has been weaving the rich tradition of Nishijin textile for over 270 years, spanning 13 generations. From its inception, Konkan Shoten has specialized in Kinran, a luxurious textile that uses golden threads to create intricate patterns. Yukio-san and Masami-san, the current custodians, continue this proud tradition. Stepping through their entrance, one is immediately enchanted by the traditional Japanese architecture and the meticulously maintained garden. As you walk through the garden and glance inside, you are greeted by shelves filled with stunning Nishijin fabrics, evoking a sense of wonder and excitement. Konkan Shoten holds dear a philosophy passed down from their founders: "Whether a customer buys a small piece of cloth or many, we cherish them equally." This enduring belief, encapsulated in their family motto, "Even an inch of cloth is our valued customer," has been the cornerstone of their legacy. When we were invited by yurie to select the fabric for our pen cases, we experienced this heartfelt hospitality firsthand. It's this deep-seated commitment that has enabled them to remain leaders in the Kinran tradition for so long. At Komorebi, we are honored to collaborate with Konkan Shoten and yurie to bring this extraordinary heritage to the world. Our goal is to share the beauty of their craft and help you discover stationery that you will cherish for a lifetime.

From its inception, Konkan Shoten has specialized in Kinran, a luxurious textile that uses golden threads to create intricate patterns. Yukio-san and Masami-san, the current custodians, continue this proud tradition. Stepping through their entrance, one is immediately enchanted by the traditional Japanese architecture and the meticulously maintained garden. As you walk through the garden and glance inside, you are greeted by shelves filled with stunning Nishijin fabrics, evoking a sense of wonder and excitement. Konkan Shoten holds dear a philosophy passed down from their founders: "Whether a customer buys a small piece of cloth or many, we cherish them equally." This enduring belief, encapsulated in their family motto, "Even an inch of cloth is our valued customer," has been the cornerstone of their legacy. When we were invited by yurie to select the fabric for our pen cases, we experienced this heartfelt hospitality firsthand. It's this deep-seated commitment that has enabled them to remain leaders in the Kinran tradition for so long. At Komorebi, we are honored to collaborate with Konkan Shoten and yurie to bring this extraordinary heritage to the world. Our goal is to share the beauty of their craft and help you discover stationery that you will cherish for a lifetime.

Discover Hakuichi's Artistry Hakuichi, located in Kanazawa City in Ishikawa Prefecture — where 99% of Japan's gold leaf is produced — has been a pioneering force in gold leaf artistry since 1975. As a leader in this field, Hakuichi has been crucial in popularizing and preserving this traditional Japanese craft globally. Kanazawa's fame in gold leaf production is deeply rooted in its artisans' exceptional craftsmanship, characterized by precision and perseverance. In the Edo period, when gold beating was restricted outside Edo (Tokyo) and Kyoto, Kanazawa's artisans refined their craft under challenging conditions. This period of adversity ultimately led to the region becoming renowned for its exquisite gold leaf. With each piece of gold leaf stationery, Hakuichi brings a touch of Kanazawa's elegance to your desk. These products are not merely tools for writing but embody a rich legacy of Japanese artistry. High-quality gold in their stationery transforms every writing experience into a luxurious encounter, making these items highly valued among Japanese traditional crafts and a splendid addition for any stationery enthusiast.

title: Sakura Tatami Pen Tray
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Filing & Organization > Desk Organizers
description: In a delightful collaboration with Sasasho from Oita Prefecture, who has been lovingly handcrafting tatami mats for local homes for 40 years, we've developed this tatami pen tray that whispers tales of Japanese charm. Adorned with the 'Sakura' (cherry blossom) pattern, a symbol of Japan's fleeting beauty, this pen tray reflects the delicate and graceful nature of the cherry blossoms. Each petal represents the transient yet breathtaking moments in life, embodying the spirit of renewal and the celebration of new beginnings. Tatami mats have graced Japanese estates since the Heian period (8th to 12th centuries), signifying wealth among nobles and warriors. Today, they continue to be a staple in Japanese homes and temples, perhaps you've even seen them on your travels. The scent of rush grass from tatami is wonderfully comforting, and their softness is unmatched for rest and relaxation. This has made tatami rooms a cherished feature in even the most modern Japanese homes. Many have noticed tatami's golden hue, but it begins as a vibrant green, gracefully maturing with sunlight. Our pen tray invites you to savor this natural aging process, bringing a piece of Japanese life to your desk, as the colors and textures gently shift over time. Explore our collection of Japanese pen trays, where timeless craftsmanship meets modern elegance. Details Brand: Sasasho Quantity: 1 pen tray Size: 7.8×3.9×0.4inches / 20×10×1cm Weight: 3.9oz / 110g Material: Rush Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Discover Sasasho For over 40 years, Sasasho has played a pivotal role in supporting the local homes and businesses, delivering tatami that underpins the regional and Japanese way of life and culture. Yasuyuki Sasaki, the current president, proudly represents the second generation at the helm of this essential local enterprise. Sasasho isn't just about making tatami; they're also the local heroes when it comes to repairing fusuma, shoji, and mosquito screens, ensuring every Japanese home feels just right. Embracing tradition while welcoming innovation, they've ventured into crafting tatami mats with unique designs and safe tatami blocks for kids, proving that tradition can evolve and still stay true to its roots. For those enchanted by Japanese culture and lifestyle, Sasasho offers a glimpse into how traditional craftsmanship like theirs continues to adapt and thrive. It's a testament to how they're blending the old with the new, inviting us all to appreciate the craftsmanship that has supported Japanese homes and culture for decades.

description: The Chidori Ivory Glass Dip Pen with Pen Rest is a refined addition to any Japanese stationery collection, crafted in soft ivory tones that evoke the gentle ripples and sparkling bubbles of the sea. Gabi Kobo: Modern Craft, Timeless Tradition Gabi Kobo is a studio where art meets tradition under the skilled hands of Tomoya Sakagami. The studio's name derives from the elegant 'Gabizan' plant, blending with Sakagami's own name to symbolize the beauty and grace of his creations. Each glass pen crafted by Tomoya is not just a tool but a piece of art, reflecting his commitment to aesthetics and functionality. He started in burner work out of curiosity after stumbling upon a beginner's kit in a department store, and his passion grew as he explored and mastered the craft independently. Tomoya's design choices are deeply influenced by the beauty and continuity of traditional Japanese patterns. He chooses motifs such as hemp leaves and the Shippo Tsunagi not merely for their traditional value but for their stunning visual appeal and the intricate perspectives they offer. His innovative method involves connecting multiple elongated beads, allowing for detailed pattern work without compromising the integrity of the glass. This technique, along with his use of chemical reactions in the glass to create unique color effects, sets his pens apart in both beauty and construction.

title: Plum Shippo Ware Medium Pen Tray
google_product_category: Office Supplies > Office Instruments > Writing & Drawing Instruments
description: This pen tray, adorned with charming plum blossoms against a beautiful golden background, is a traditional Japanese craft known as Shippo ware (Japanese cloisonné). Each design is meticulously hand-painted with glassy enamel on copper and fired at a high temperature of around 800 degrees, resulting in colors that are more vivid than those of ceramics. Because it is made from glass, the colors and patterns never fade, allowing it to be passed down from generation to generation. The tray is sized to comfortably hold one pen with some space to spare, making it perfect for adding a touch of Japanese elegance and vibrancy to your desk. Imagining this piece of Shippo ware, with its origins dating back to the 6th or 7th century, gracing your desk overseas is truly wonderful. In Japan, plum blossoms are considered very auspicious. They are among the first flowers to bloom in the year and symbolize strong vitality, often associated with warding off illness and wishing for longevity. If you are looking for a gift for someone who appreciates craftsmanship, Japanese tradition, stationery, or journaling, this tray is highly recommended. Explore our collection of Japanese pen trays, where timeless craftsmanship meets modern elegance. Details Size: 2.75" × 9.4" (7 cm × 24 cm) Weight: 5.7 oz (163 g) Material: Glass on Copper Note: Each piece is uniquely handmade, so expect some individual differences. Japanese Art of Copper and Glass by Saikosha Saikosha, the largest kiln producing Shippo ware in Japan, has made significant contributions to the long history of Shippo ware, which dates back to the 6th or 7th century. Their mission is to "continuously challenge the spirit of bringing tradition to life in the modern era." Despite the gradual decline in demand for traditional crafts, they strive to preserve the beauty of Shippo ware for contemporary times. They create various items, including vases, plates, pen trays, and accessories, that are cherished by modern audiences. Compared to other famous traditional crafts like Wajima lacquerware, Shippo ware has become less well-known in Japan. However, its vivid colors and unfading beauty, achieved through its glass enamel, have garnered a dedicated following, and it is often sold in high-end department stores. We invite you to enjoy this beauty in your country, right on your desk.

The Artist Behind the Glass Pen: Megumi Kitahashi Megumi Kitahashi is a glass artist with a unique background. She was once a postal worker. During her time at the post office, she cherished the connections she built with the elderly in her community through letters. This experience deepened her appreciation for the warmth of handwritten communication and the importance of human connections, ultimately inspiring her to embark on a journey into glass pen craftsmanship. Thus, Glass Studio Menmeru was born. The name “Menmeru” comes from a Nagano dialect phrase meaning “just stopping by for a visit,” reflecting the warmth and connection that define Kitahashi’s work. Her glass pens are an invitation to rediscover the joy of writing. With roots in traditional letter culture and infused with delicate glass artistry, her creations carry a heartfelt touch. Now available at Komorebi Stationery, her pens bring a sense of warmth and nostalgia to every stroke.

Details Brand: Kamiiso Quantity: 1 Roll Size: 0.6" × 23" (15mm × 7m) Material: Iyo Washi (Iyo Japanese Paper) Kamiiso: A Century of Washi Excellence Kamiiso, nestled in the heart of Shikoku Chuo City, Ehime Prefecture, has been a bastion of traditional Iyo Washi paper since its foundation in 1926. What began as a wholesaler delivering the allure of Iyo Washi across Japan has, over the decades, blossomed into an esteemed manufacturer, especially after expanding into the production of specialized paper for kimono storage around 30 years post-establishment. Adapting to the changing eras and the evolving lifestyles of the Japanese people, Kamiiso has diversified its offerings. The company now energetically produces a range of paper products, including table mats, coasters, and notably, their much-loved masking tapes. In an era where many washi tapes no longer contain real washi (Japanese paper), Kamiiso stands apart. They apply a century's worth of spun techniques and steadfast quality to their Washi Tapes, captivating stationery enthusiasts worldwide with their genuine dedication to this authentic Japanese craft.

description: The Kitchen Iyo Washi Sticker Sheet brings warmth and charm to your stationery with beautifully crafted torn-paper collage designs of everyday kitchen essentials. Featuring teapots, bread, fresh produce, utensils, and cozy home-cooked meals, each sticker evokes the simple joy of cooking and sharing food. Made with authentic Iyo washi paper, these stickers blend seamlessly onto journals, planners, and recipe books. The soft textures and natural color palette create a warm and inviting aesthetic, perfect for adding a homely touch to your creative projects. Whether you're decorating a food diary, personalizing recipe cards, or adding a culinary theme to your journal, these washi stickers are a delightful addition to any collection. Browse our Japanese washi stickers, made for charm and creativity. Details Size (Sheet): 6.9" × 3.5" (175mm × 90mm) Material: Iyo Washi (Iyo Japanese Paper) Brand: Kamiiso Kamiiso: A Century of Washi Excellence Kamiiso, nestled in the heart of Shikoku Chuo City, Ehime Prefecture, has been a bastion of traditional Iyo Washi paper since its foundation in 1926. What began as a wholesaler delivering the allure of Iyo Washi across Japan has, over the decades, blossomed into an esteemed manufacturer, especially after expanding into the production of specialized paper for kimono storage around 30 years post-establishment. Today, Shikoku Chuo City is revered as Japan's leading hub for paper goods, thanks in no small part to Kamiiso's dedication to spreading this time-honored tradition. Adapting to the changing eras and the evolving lifestyles of the Japanese people, Kamiiso has diversified its offerings. The company now energetically produces a range of paper products, including table mats, coasters, and notably, their much-loved masking tapes. In an era where many washi tapes no longer contain real washi (Japanese paper), Kamiiso stands apart. They apply a century's worth of spun techniques and steadfast quality to their Washi Tapes, captivating stationery enthusiasts worldwide with their genuine dedication to this authentic Japanese craft.

title: Tsubame Fools Ink Color Swatch Cards
google_product_category: Office Supplies > General Office Supplies > Paper Products > Notebooks & Notepads
description: The Tsubame Note Ink Color Swatch Cards are a premium addition to the world of Japanese stationery, meticulously crafted for fountain pen and glass pen ink enthusiasts. Utilizing Tsubame's renowned ultra-thin foolscap paper, these color swatch cards accurately capture the true hues and characteristics of various inks, including those with shimmer properties. Designed for both organization and portability, these color swatches allow users to systematically catalog their ink collections. The elegant packaging, available in refined navy or red, adds a touch of sophistication to your collection. With the Tsubame Note Ink Collection Cards, documenting and comparing the nuances of your fountain pen inks becomes an enjoyable and efficient experience, enhancing the art of writing and ink selection. Details Size: 3.6" x 2.2" (9.1 cm x 5.5 cm) Number of Cards: 150 Paper Type: Tsubame Neutral Fools Paper Weight: 81.4 gsm Weight: 3.2 oz (90 g) Brand: Tsubame Note Why Tsubame Note Is a Staple Among Japanese Notebook Enthusiasts Tsubame Note Co., Ltd. is a long-established notebook manufacturer founded in Tokyo in 1947. In the post-war era, the company adopted high-quality foolscap paper to create superior notebooks, offering a smooth writing experience that is ideal for fountain pens. Its classic design was unexpectedly suggested by a fortune teller who visited one day. The company name originates from a former sales representative, Mr. Tsubame, as customers fondly referred to the notebooks as "Tsubame’s notebooks." Upholding craftsmanship and quality, Tsubame Note continues to be cherished by writing enthusiasts worldwide.

title: Tsubame Fools Cream Notebook
google_product_category: Office Supplies > General Office Supplies > Paper Products > Notebooks & Notepads
description: The Tsubame Cream Notebook is a distinguished addition to Japanese stationery, combining classic design with exceptional writing comfort. As a premium Japanese notebook, it differs from standard Tsubame notebooks that use white foolscap paper, featuring soft, warm cream-colored pages. This subtle hue is gentle on the eyes, reducing fatigue during extended writing sessions. Maintaining Tsubame's signature smooth writing experience, the cream paper pairs excellently with various writing instruments, including fountain pens and ballpoint pens. The paper is designed to resist ink bleeding and show-through, ensuring a seamless writing experience. Additionally, being acid-free, it is ideal for long-term preservation, making it perfect for journals and record-keeping. The notebook's design upholds Tsubame's traditional aesthetic, featuring a chic cover that complements the cream-colored pages, imparting an elegant and refined impression. Its versatility makes it suitable for various settings, from business meetings to casual café note-taking. For those seeking a premium notebook, the Tsubame Cream Notebook offers a simple yet profound joy in writing, reflecting meticulous craftsmanship. Explore our Japanese notebooks collection, crafted for exceptional writing and creative sketching. Details Size: A5 / B5 Sheet Type: Blank / Grid / Lined Pages: 64 Pages (32 Sheets) Paper Type: Tsubame Neutral Fools Paper Weight: 81.4 gsm Weight: A5 - 4.9 oz (140 g) / B5 -3.3 oz (95 g) Brand: Tsubame Note Why Tsubame Note Is a Staple Among Japanese Notebook Enthusiasts Tsubame Note Co., Ltd. is a long-established notebook manufacturer founded in Tokyo in 1947. In the post-war era, the company adopted high-quality foolscap paper to create superior notebooks, offering a smooth writing experience that is ideal for fountain pens. Its classic design was unexpectedly suggested by a fortune teller who visited one day. The company name originates from a former sales representative, Mr. Tsubame, as customers fondly referred to the notebooks as "Tsubame’s notebooks." Upholding craftsmanship and quality, Tsubame Note continues to be cherished by writing enthusiasts worldwide.

The paper resists bleeding and show-through, allowing for comfortable writing on both sides. Additionally, it is acid-free, ensuring archival quality, so that important notes and records can be preserved for a lifetime. Another defining characteristic of the Tsubame Notebook is its timeless, classic design. Featuring a simple yet elegant gray cover with intricate embellishments, its appearance has remained virtually unchanged for decades. This understated sophistication has made it a favorite across generations, appealing to students, professionals, writers, and researchers alike. The craftsmanship behind this Japanese notebook extends to the meticulously drawn ruling lines. Using a traditional technique called keiki (ruling), these lines are printed with water-based ink, ensuring smooth, uninterrupted writing from edge to edge. The result is a notebook that not only looks beautiful but also enhances the writing experience with its thoughtful details. More than just a notebook, the Tsubame University Notebook is designed to elevate the joy of writing. Whether for daily notes, creative projects, or academic pursuits, it offers a writing surface that feels as refined as the words it holds. Gabi Kobo: Modern Craft, Timeless Tradition Gabi Kobo is a studio where art meets tradition under the skilled hands of Tomoya Sakagami. The studio's name derives from the elegant 'Gabizan' plant, blending with Sakagami's own name to symbolize the beauty and grace of his creations. Each glass pen crafted by Tomoya is not just a tool but a piece of art, reflecting his commitment to aesthetics and functionality. He started in burner work out of curiosity after stumbling upon a beginner's kit in a department store, and his passion grew as he explored and mastered the craft independently. Tomoya's design choices are deeply influenced by the beauty and continuity of traditional Japanese patterns. He chooses motifs such as hemp leaves and the Shippo Tsunagi not merely for their traditional value but for their stunning visual appeal and the intricate perspectives they offer. His innovative method involves connecting multiple elongated beads, allowing for detailed pattern work without compromising the integrity of the glass. This technique, along with his use of chemical reactions in the glass to create unique color effects, sets his pens apart in both beauty and construction.

His innovative method involves connecting multiple elongated beads, allowing for detailed pattern work without compromising the integrity of the glass. This technique, along with his use of chemical reactions in the glass to create unique color effects, sets his pens apart in both beauty and construction.

title: Sanzen Tomoe River FP Writing Pad
google_product_category: Office Supplies > General Office Supplies > Paper Products > Notebooks & Notepads
description: Tomoe River FP Writing Pad is a premium Japanese stationery highly acclaimed among fountain pen and calligraphy enthusiasts. The pad features Tomoe River FP paper, renowned for its exceptional smoothness and resistance to ink bleed-through, ensuring a delightful writing experience. The cover is printed with a logo mark co-created with Tomoe River Paper Co., Ltd., signifying its authenticity. The pad is available in A4 and A5 sizes, and in white and cream paper colors, allowing users to select according to their preferences and writing needs. Each pad contains 100 sheets, providing ample paper for various writing tasks. Whether for everyday writing, calligraphy practice, or as a versatile material for custom projects, Tomoe River FP Writing Pad offers the quality and reliability expected from fine Japanese stationery. Check out our other writing pads for more designs and paper styles you might love. Details Size: A4 / A5 Number of sheets: 100 Sheet Type: Blank Paper Type: Tomoe River FP Paper Weight: 52 gsm Weight: 327 oz (11.5 g) Brand: paper paper The History of Tomoe River FP Writing Pad’s Maker Paper Paper is a brand created by SAKAE Technical Paper. Its history dates back to 1940. SAKAE has gone through several successions and company splits. It has also been an agent for Tomoe River, a brand with a global fanbase. Tomoe River’s success likely owes much to SAKAE’s achievements. Since 2014, SAKAE has produced and sold Tomoe RiverFP notebooks using Tomoe River paper. Even after Tomoegawa transferred the rights to Sanzen Seishi, SAKAE continued production using the new Tomoe River. The mission of Paper Paper is to explore the appeal of paper and expand possibilities between people and paper. Beyond Tomoe River, they continue to develop new papers like Iroful, supporting stationery lovers worldwide.
